topic.what: Threat-oriented digital twinning methodology for cybersecurity evaluation of learning-enabled autonomous platforms
topic.how: Constructs modular twin with separated sensing/autonomy/control domains, confidence-gated multi-modal perception, explicit communication trust boundaries, and runtime hold-safe behavior to enable observable security tests for spoofing/replay/injection/degraded-sensing/adversarial-ML attacks
topic.why_matters: Open unclassified research on secure autonomy is constrained by limited access to operational platforms and contested infrastructure; this provides a reproducible scaffold that translates threat analysis into measurable assurance tests across UAV and space domains

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of attack surface categories testable via twin architecture
characteristics.evidence: Section IV evaluation covers communication-path attacks perception stress and runtime-assurance behavior
characteristics.context: Initial evaluation focused on cross-layer trust failures and bounded operational effects under representative faults

characteristics.what_it_measures: Instrumentation depth for security-relevant state transitions
characteristics.value: 5+ stages instrumented (perception fusion communication operator-interaction state-transition)
characteristics.evidence: Each major stage emits structured logs and machine-consumable summaries enabling provenance tracking
characteristics.context: Turns simulator into security testbed exposing whether spoofed inputs were filtered confidence collapsed before action or hold-safe triggered for correct reason

characteristics.what_itasures: Isolation between sensing autonomy and control domains
characteristics.evidence: Sensor simulation host autonomy-core host control gateway host with separate communication paths
characteristics.context: Software analogue of modular separation found in real autonomy programs preserving typed interfaces and typed nodes

characteristics.value: explicit degraded-mode logic with bounded fallback behavior
characteristics.evidence: State machine includes idle ready prepare-to-act restricted hold-safe modes; confidence sensor-communication health influence transitions
characteristics.context: System responds to trust decay not just detects anomalies per NIST cyber-resilience guidance

characteristics.evidence: Threat modeling covers spoofing tampering information-disclosure DoS privilege-escalation at interface level with ATLAS complementing ATT&CK for ML pipelines
characteristics.context: Comprehensive adversary vocabulary applied to ML-enabled autonomy pipeline

good_for: security researchers evaluating UAV/ground autonomous platforms
academic studies requiring open-source reproducible security testbeds
adversarial-ML stress testing on learning-enabled perception
not_for: High-fidelity environmental simulation or perception accuracy benchmarking
Classified or operational platform security evaluation
Industrial control systems or manufacturing environments
Single-attack-vector isolated testing without system context
requires: Multi-modal sensor setup (RGB depth LiDAR thermal)
Controlled test environment for spoofing/replay injection
Gazebo simulation infrastructure for sensor feeds

novelty: Threat-oriented digital twinning methodology translating threat analysis into observable controllable security tests
Confidence-gated multi-modal perception with cross-sensor validation preventing direct authority of ML detections
Explicit trust boundary separation between telemetry (DTLS) and command (MQTT/TLS) traffic with post-decryption validation
State-machine-based runtime assurance with health-aware transitions and operator-governable hold-safe modes
Open-source autonomy twin exposing track starvation stale-display persistence and adversarial thermovisual perturbation failure modes
open_problems: Full ATLAS/ATT&CK coverage across all pipeline stages not yet demonstrated
Scalability to multi-agent or cooperative autonomy scenarios unvalidated
Broader adversarial-ML perturbation types beyond thermovisual not systematically tested
Long-duration stress testing and fatigue effects on security assurances not evaluated
Extension to space systems with intermittent high-latency links needs architectural hardening
